info.name: Expense Tracker — Complete API
info.description:
Single collection covering every endpoint in the Expense Tracker Spring Boot application.

Features:
- Auth (register, login, roles, assign-roles, update base currency)
- Expenses (CRUD + search + filters + tag filtering + multi-currency)
- Receipts (batch upload to S3, create-expense-with-receipts, list, delete — pre-signed URLs)
- Categories (admin global + user-specific)
- Budgets (CRUD + status)
- Recurring Expenses (CRUD)
- Reports (summary, category-wise, date-wise, custom, monthly trends, budget performance, insights, top-expenses, export CSV/PDF)
- Tags (CRUD, per-user, many-to-many with expenses)
- Exchange Rates (list rates, convert, manual sync — open.er-api.com, daily auto-sync at 01:00)
- Audit Logs (all, mine, by-user, by-entity, by-action)
- Notifications (list, unread, mark-read, delete)
- Dashboard

Setup:
1. Import this collection.
2. Set collection variable base_url to your server (default: http://localhost:8081).
3. Run Login under Auth - the test script auto-saves the JWT to token.
4. All other requests inherit Bearer auth from the collection level.
5. Run 'Sync Exchange Rates' once (ADMIN) to load rates from open.er-api.com before using multi-currency expenses.
6. For receipt uploads: in Body → form-data, use key 'files' with Type set to File. Add multiple rows with the same key to upload multiple files at once.

variable.description: Auto-filled by the Login request test script
Auto-filled by Create Expense test script
Auto-filled by Create Global Category test script
Auto-filled by Get All Notifications test script
Auto-filled by Create User Category test script
Auto-filled by Create Budget test script

Used by SUPER_ADMIN endpoints that target another user

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Registration, login, profile, roles management.
Public: register, login, roles.
Authenticated: me.
SUPER_ADMIN: assign-roles.

request.description: Create a new user account. Returns JWT token + user info.

exec: console.warn('Login failed — no token in response');

request.description: Authenticate and receive a JWT. The test script auto-saves the token to the `token` collection variable. Uses SUPER_ADMIN credentials so all endpoints work correctly.
Returns the authenticated user's email and authorities from the JWT.
Returns all available role names. Public endpoint.
Assign one or more roles to a user. Requires SUPER_ADMIN role.

Update the authenticated user's base currency. All future expenses will have their amountInBase computed by converting from the expense currency to this base. Must be a valid 3-letter ISO 4217 code (e.g. INR, USD, EUR, GBP).

item.name: Expenses
item.description: Full CRUD for expenses plus search and filter endpoints. All require authentication.

request.description: Returns paginated expenses for the authenticated user, sorted by createdAt DESC.

exec: // Fetch first available category to use a valid categoryId

request.description: Create a new expense. `userId` is overridden by the server with the authenticated user's ID. Budget check runs before save.

response.name: 400 Budget Exceeded

request.description: Get a single expense by ID. Returns 404 if it doesn't belong to the authenticated user.
Update an existing expense. Only the owner can update.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Permanently delete an expense. Only the owner can delete.
Full-text search on expense title for the authenticated user.
Returns all expenses in the given category for the authenticated user.
Returns expenses within the given amount range for the authenticated user.
Returns expenses within the given date-time range for the authenticated user.
Multi-criteria paginated filter. All body fields are optional.

item.name: Categories — Admin
item.description: Manage global (system-wide) categories. Requires ADMIN or SUPER_ADMIN role.

request.description: List all global (system-wide) categories. Requires ADMIN role.
Creates a global category visible to all users. Requires ADMIN role. Pre-request generates unique name; test script auto-saves category_id.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Update a global category by ID. Requires ADMIN role. Uses the timestamp name generated by Create to avoid name conflicts.
Delete a global category by ID. Requires ADMIN role.

item.name: Categories — User
item.description: Manage user-specific categories. Requires authentication (any logged-in user).

request.description: Returns all categories available to the authenticated user (global + own user-specific).

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Create a user-specific category. Requires authentication. Pre-request generates unique name; test script auto-saves user_category_id.
Update a user-specific category. Requires authentication. Uses user_category_id auto-saved by Create User Category.
Delete a user-specific category. Requires authentication. Returns 400 if category is in use by expenses, budgets, or recurring expenses.

item.name: Budgets
item.description: Create and manage spending budgets per category or overall. All require authentication.

request.description: Create a new overall budget (no category). `categoryId` is optional — null creates an overall spending budget. `period`: DAILY | WEEKLY | MONTHLY | YEARLY. `alertThreshold`: fraction (0.0–1.0) at which a BUDGET_ALERT notification fires. Test script auto-saves budget_id.
Returns all active, non-deleted budgets for the authenticated user with real-time spent/remaining amounts.
Returns real-time budget utilisation for a single budget: spent, remaining, percentUsed, isOverBudget, isNearLimit.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "status": true,
  "message": "Budget status fetched successfully",
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "categoryId": 1,
    "categoryName": "Food",
    "limit": 10000.0,
    "spent": 6500.0,
    "remaining": 3500.0,
    "percentUsed": 65.0,
    "isOverBudget": false,
    "isNearLimit": false
  }
}

request.description: Partially update a budget. All fields are optional — only provided fields are changed.
Soft-delete a budget (sets deletedAt timestamp). Audit logged.

item.name: Recurring Expenses
item.description: Schedule expenses that auto-repeat on a frequency. Processed daily at 00:05. All require authentication.

exec: // Fetch first available category to use a valid categoryId

request.description: Create a recurring expense. Pre-request fetches first available category. `frequency`: DAILY | WEEKLY | MONTHLY | YEARLY. `startDate` is the first `nextDueDate`. Test script auto-saves recurring_id.

response.name: 201 Created
response.body:
{
  "status": true,
  "message": "Recurring expense created successfully",
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "title": "Netflix Subscription",
    "amount": 649.0,
    "categoryId": 1,
    "categoryName": "Entertainment",
    "frequency": "MONTHLY",
    "nextDueDate": "2026-03-16",
    "endDate": null,
    "isActive": true,
    "note": "Streaming subscription",
    "createdAt": "2026-03-15T10:00:00"
  }
}

request.description: Returns all active, non-deleted recurring expenses for the authenticated user.
Get a single recurring expense by ID. Returns 404 if not owned by the authenticated user.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Netflix (4K)",
  "amount": 799.00,
  "categoryId": null,
  "frequency": null,
  "nextDueDate": null,
  "endDate": "2026-12-31",
  "isActive": true,
  "note": "Upgraded to 4K plan"
}

request.description: Partially update a recurring expense. All fields are optional.
Soft-delete a recurring expense. Audit logged.

item.name: Reports
item.description: Expense aggregation reports. Requires SUPER_ADMIN role.

request.description: Returns total expense amount and record count for the authenticated user (all time).
Returns total spending grouped by category for the authenticated user.
Returns daily expense totals for the authenticated user.
Returns filtered expenses matching the given criteria. All body fields are optional.

query.description: Number of past months to include (1–24, default 6)

request.description: Returns month-over-month spending totals for the last N calendar months. Each month includes totalAmount, expenseCount, and a category breakdown with percentages. Months with zero spending are included with 0 values. Requires SUPER_ADMIN role.

Returns real-time budget vs actual spending for every active budget. Each entry has: limit, spent, remaining, percentUsed, isOverBudget, isNearLimit, and a status string (ON_TRACK | NEAR_LIMIT | EXCEEDED). Requires SUPER_ADMIN role.

response.name: 200 OK — Budget Performance
response.body:
{
  "status": true,
  "message": "Budget performance report fetched",
  "data": [
    {
      "budgetId": 1,
      "categoryId": 1,
      "categoryName": "Food",
      "period": "MONTHLY",
      "budgetLimit": 10000.0,
      "spent": 8500.0,
      "remaining": 1500.0,
      "percentUsed": 85.0,
      "isOverBudget": false,
      "isNearLimit": true,
      "status": "NEAR_LIMIT"
    },
    {
      "budgetId": 2,
      "categoryId": null,
      "categoryName": null,
      "period": "MONTHLY",
      "budgetLimit": 30000.0,
      "spent": 12500.0,
      "remaining": 17500.0,
      "percentUsed": 41.67,
      "isOverBudget": false,
      "isNearLimit": false,
      "status": "ON_TRACK"
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Returns a spending insight snapshot: month-over-month change, spending velocity (INCREASING/DECREASING/STABLE), average daily spend (last 30 days), highest spend day, biggest single expense, and most-used category.

query.description: Number of top expenses to return (1-100, default 10)

request.description: Returns the top N expenses sorted by amount descending. Optional categoryId filter. limit is capped between 1 and 100.
Download all expenses as a CSV file. Response is a file attachment (text/csv).
Download all expenses as a styled PDF file. Response is a file attachment (application/pdf).

item.name: Tags
item.description: Per-user tags (labels) that can be attached to expenses. Many-to-many relationship. Requires authentication.

request.description: Create a new tag for the authenticated user. Name must be unique per user. Color must be a valid hex code (e.g. #6366f1). Returns 201 on success. Saves tag_id to collection variable.
Returns all tags belonging to the authenticated user.
Returns a single tag by ID. Returns 404 if the tag does not belong to the authenticated user.
Update the name and/or color of an existing tag. Returns 409 if the new name conflicts with another tag owned by the same user.
Delete a tag. Automatically removed from all associated expenses via CASCADE on expense_tags. Returns 404 if not found or not owned by caller.

item.name: Exchange Rates
item.description: Multi-currency support. Rates are fetched from open.er-api.com daily (01:00 AM) with USD as the pivot base. Use GET /convert to convert any amount between currencies.

request.description: Returns all stored exchange rates. All rates use USD as the pivot base: rate = how many target units equal 1 USD. Example: { baseCurrency: 'USD', targetCurrency: 'INR', rate: 83.5 } means 1 USD = 83.5 INR.

query.description: Source currency ISO 4217 code (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP)

request.description: Convert an amount from one currency to another using stored exchange rates. Returns { fromCurrency, toCurrency, originalAmount, convertedAmount }.
Manually trigger a full exchange rate sync from open.er-api.com. Requires ADMIN role. Normally runs automatically daily at 01:00 AM. Returns { ratesUpdated: N }.

item.name: Audit Logs
item.description:
Read-only audit trail of all entity changes.
GET /       → ADMIN
GET /me     → any authenticated user
GET /user   → SUPER_ADMIN
GET /entity → ADMIN
GET /action → ADMIN

request.description: Returns all audit logs paginated. Requires ADMIN role.
Returns audit logs for the authenticated user only. Available to any authenticated user.
Returns audit logs for any user by ID. Requires SUPER_ADMIN role.
Returns full change history for a specific entity. `entity_type`: Expense | Budget | RecurringExpense | Category. Requires ADMIN role.

Filter audit logs by action type. `audit_action` values: EXPENSE_CREATED | EXPENSE_UPDATED | EXPENSE_DELETED | EXPENSE_AUTO_CREATED | BUDGET_CREATED | BUDGET_UPDATED | BUDGET_DELETED | RECURRING_EXPENSE_CREATED | RECURRING_EXPENSE_UPDATED | RECURRING_EXPENSE_DELETED | RECURRING_EXPENSE_PROCESSED | CATEGORY_CREATED | CATEGORY_UPDATED | CATEGORY_DELETED | USER_REGISTERED | USER_LOGIN | ROLE_ASSIGNED | NOTIFICATION_READ | NOTIFICATION_DELETED. Requires ADMIN role.

item.name: Notifications
item.description: In-app notifications triggered by budget alerts, budget exceeded events, and recurring expense processing. All require authentication.

exec: console.log('No notifications found — mark-read and delete will be skipped');

request.description: Returns all notifications for the authenticated user, newest first (paginated).

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Returns all unread notifications (not paginated). Use for badge display.
Returns the integer count of unread notifications. Lightweight endpoint for badge counters.

exec: console.log('No notification_id set — skipping Mark as Read');

request.description: Mark a single notification as read. Returns 404 if it doesn't belong to the authenticated user. Audit logged as NOTIFICATION_READ. Skipped automatically if no notification_id is available.
Marks all unread notifications as read in one call. Returns count of updated records.

exec: console.log('No notification_id set — skipping Delete Notification');

request.description: Permanently delete a notification. Returns 404 if it doesn't belong to the authenticated user. Audit logged as NOTIFICATION_DELETED. Skipped automatically if no notification_id is available.

item.name: Dashboard
item.description: Single aggregated financial snapshot for the authenticated user.

request.description: Returns: this-month spending summary, budget overview with utilisation, last 5 expenses, upcoming recurring expenses (next 7 days), category breakdown with percentages, and unread notification count.

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: Receipts
item.description:
Upload, list, and delete receipt files (jpg/png/pdf, max 5 MB each, max 5 per expense) stored in AWS S3. Download URLs are pre-signed with a 1-hour TTL.

For file uploads: in Body → form-data set key to 'files', change Type dropdown to File, and select your file. Add multiple rows with the same key 'files' to upload several files at once.

formdata.description: Select one or more jpg/png/pdf files (max 5 MB each). Add multiple rows with the same key 'files' to upload several at once.

request.description:
Upload one or more receipt files to S3 and attach them to an existing expense. All files are validated (type + size) before any upload begins — if one fails, nothing is uploaded. Returns List<ReceiptResponse> each with a 1-hour pre-signed downloadUrl.

Rules:
- Allowed types: image/jpeg, image/png, application/pdf
- Max 5 MB per file
- Max 5 receipts total per expense (existing + new batch must not exceed 5)

Test script auto-saves first receipt id to receipt_id variable.

formdata.description: Expense JSON — userId is overwritten by the server from the JWT token
Optional — select one or more receipt files (jpg/png/pdf). Leave empty to create expense without receipt.

request.description:
Create an expense AND attach receipts in a single API call (multipart/form-data).

Parts:
- expense (Text, Content-Type: application/json): expense fields as JSON
- files (File, optional): one or more receipt files

In Postman: for the 'expense' key click the ⋯ menu and set Content-Type to application/json. For 'files' change Type dropdown to File.

Returns ExpenseResponse. Test script auto-saves expense id to expense_id variable.
List all receipts attached to an expense. Each receipt includes a downloadUrl pointing to the /download endpoint. Only the owner of the expense can access this.
Stream the raw file bytes for a receipt. Returns the file with correct Content-Type header (image/jpeg, image/png, or application/pdf). Use receipt_id from the Get Receipts response.
Permanently delete a receipt from both S3 and the database. Audit logged as RECEIPT_DELETED. Uses expense_id and receipt_id collection variables.
